theme.premise: a lone free-diver descending a bioluminescent ocean trench at dusk, gathering pearls and sunken relics out of the dark and racing her one held breath back to the surface before the deep keeps her catch
theme.tone: serene but breath-held tense — quiet beauty laced with the dread of going too deep; the pull of 'just one more fathom'
theme.setting: a dark vertical ocean trench at dusk: fading teal surface light and godrays up top dimming to inky black, glowing sea life and sunken relics below

concept.core_loop: The diver sinks continuously while you drag to steer her left/right, grabbing drifting treasures (the deeper ones worth far more) and dodging air-draining hazards; tapping ASCEND reverses her toward the surface, and reaching the surface BANKS the dive's haul into your permanent score. The live decision every second is GREED vs AIR: pushing deeper for richer treasure spends air you will need to climb back, and the long ascent itself costs air, so 'one more relic' gains haul value (A) but gives up the safety margin (B) that gets the whole catch home. Run out of air before surfacing and you forfeit the entire UNBANKED dive. Starts shallow and calm with a generous tank; each completed dive ramps depth reached, hazard density, and air-drain rate up to a hard cap, so the safe banking depth creeps shallower and the greed tax rises.
mechanics: continuous vertical descent with horizontal drag/tap steering (Android touch + desktop mouse)
air gauge that depletes continuously and faster the deeper you are; the core resource
drifting treasures spawned by depth band, deeper bands worth multiplicatively more, collected on contact into an UNBANKED dive haul
hazards (jelly, eel) that drain a chunk of air on contact, denser deeper
ASCEND action (button + swipe up) that reverses to surfacing; reaching the surface banks the haul into permanent score and starts the next dive
loss condition: air reaches zero before surfacing -> forfeit the entire unbanked haul (soft per-dive game over), then restart the dive
HUD: depth meter, air gauge (pulses red when low), current unbanked haul value, banked total score
per-dive difficulty ramp (start depth band, hazard density, air-drain rate) with MAX_* caps
concept.art_direction: Dark vertical gradient: dim teal surface light + faint godrays at top fading to near-black depths. Bioluminescent accents are the palette — cyan and magenta glowing treasures and jellies, each drawn with the oversized low-alpha halo behind a crisp shape (the glow recipe). The diver is a clean pale silhouette with a small warm headlamp cone. Background never dead: layered depth gradient + slow parallax plankton dots drifting up + faint godrays. Motion/feedback: white flash + short screen shake when a hazard drains air; gold pulse + sparkle particle when a treasure is collected; the air bar pulses red and the vignette tightens as air runs low; rising bubble particles trail the diver. This is the visual expression of the breath-held bioluminescent-trench theme — luminous points in deep dark, not bright arcade neon.
concept.differentiation_notes: Not an endless runner or flappy clone: the hook is the push-your-luck BANKING decision (deep-dive/greed-game lineage) mapped onto a one-thumb portrait diver. Every fathom deeper is a live bet of air against treasure value, and the loss condition forfeits only the unbanked haul — so the tension is self-inflicted greed, not unfair death. The tradeoff is intrinsic to the loop (reward scales with depth, safety scales inversely), which is the differentiator over score-climbing arcade peers.

systems_added: depth ZONES (Shallows/Reef/Trench) with distinct treasure value AND distinct hazard behavior — deep predators HOME on the diver (Main._move_hazards), so deep water plays differently, not just for more points

pressure CRUSH gate: past max_safe_depth air burns CRUSH_MULT(4x) faster (DiveState.current_drain) — depth is risk, and the Pressure Rig upgrade pushes the crush line deeper (legible on-screen safe-depth line)

per-dive COMMISSION: an order fillable only by treasures from a target zone (collect counts zone>=commission_zone); banking a filled order pays a bonus and advances the target deeper — a concrete reason to descend, not a multiplier
lantern darkness: deep zones are dark (Main._light_factor); treasure is unseeable outside lamp_range, so the Lantern upgrade changes what you can do
repurposed upgrades as decision-changers: Rig (zone access), Lantern (see the deep), Tank (air budget), Fins (sharper steering to dodge homing predators)
depth_pass.notes: RE-DEEPEN that corrects the prior run-meta pass (which bolted an upgrade shop onto a one-decision core — the core-before-meta mistake). Owner playtest rejected v1 as shallow: depth was pure value-multiplier and upgrades barely mattered. Applied the upgraded deepen diagnostic (progression-payoff + upgrade-coherence + core-before-meta) and made depth a DESTINATION: zones gated by the crush, a commission that requires reaching them, darkness the lantern fights, and homing deep predators. INDEPENDENT design-depth audit verdict (required new step, fresh subagent, no knowledge of intent): 'DEEPER, not just bigger — the commission + crush + zone-homing + darkness make depth a destination with distinct verbs, clearing the depth-as-multiplier trap.' The audit caught one real weakness the implementer missed — Fins was a flat ~17% ascend nudge — which was then fixed (Fins now sharpens steering to dodge the deep's homing predators, a felt decision-relevant effect). Surfaced frozen-logic changes (surface-don't-swallow): DiveState.collect signature changed to (zone); treasure value is now zone-based not depth-based; the Rebreather upgrade was replaced by the Rig; MAX_AIR stays dynamic. selftest fully rewritten (12 stages: zones, crush, rig gating, commission fill+forfeit, upgrade coherence, persistence); UITEST OK retained. status unchanged (validated) — deepen does not advance status.
